name: MrBang21
content: In Nordic and broader European medical standards, infant circumcision is not a medically indicated procedure. A healthy newborn male does not require removal of foreskin tissue, and being born with a penis is not a medical indication for surgery.

[Infant circumcision](https://mrbang21.github.io/human/#male_circ_infant) is a non-therapeutic surgical intervention performed on healthy tissue in a non-consenting child. Many European medical perspectives describe routine infant circumcision as unnecessary, with some Nordic medical discourse explicitly classifying it as genital mutilation.
I live in Denmark, where [circumcision](https://mrbang21.github.io/human/#male_circumcision) is rare outside specific religious contexts and is not part of standard pediatric practice.

Some circumcised men report [loss of sensation](https://mrbang21.github.io/human/#male_circ_loss_of_sensitivity), altered sexual experience, or psychological distress, while others report neutrality or acceptance.
This collection exists to document lived experiences of circumcision without institutional or ideological filtering.
Every testimony belongs to a real person with a unique perspective. Some were circumcised as infants, others later in life, and some are partners or family members affected indirectly.
**Submit Your Own Testimony**
If circumcision has affected you or someone close to you, you can share your story.
You can contact me through [@MrBang21](https://x.com/MrBang21).
Submissions can be any length and may include personal experience, reflections, or views on infant circumcision and genital autonomy.

**Privacy Options**
You choose how your testimony is published:

***Option 1: Completely Anonymous***
No name, profile, or identifying information will be shown.

***Option 2: Named***
A name or nickname will be displayed.

***Option 3: Linked Profile***
A link to your social media profile can be included if you choose.
Country or general location (for example Denmark, Germany, United States) may be included if you want contextual background while remaining otherwise anonymous.
No testimony is published without explicit consent.
*"Every testimony reflects a lived reality. Every lived reality deserves to be recorded with honesty."*

name: Nikorasu85
content: I complain!
I will always wish it were not done to me.
It caused years of confusion. I now know what I'm missing and I will never get to experience what an uncut man gets to.
Someone else got to decide how much sensation and pleasure I get to feel in life.

name: Fokam Kuate Hervé
content: Male genital mutilation (circumcision) is legal here. Female genital mutilation is illegal.
I did not realize I had been circumcised until I was 30 years old. Discovering that a part of my body had been permanently removed without my knowledge or consent was shocking.

When I confronted my parents, they gave me every justification imaginable. Tradition. Religion. HIV prevention. Claims that women prefer it. Even accusations that I had been "brainwashed" by France. Excuse after excuse, anything except acknowledging what had been done to me.
I demanded one of two things: a sincere apology or that I would remove them from my life. They knelt and asked for forgiveness, and for a time I believed they genuinely regretted their decision.
Later, I realized nothing had really changed.

Whenever I tried to discuss circumcision, they ignored me. They allowed their financially dependent son to have their grandchildren circumcised. They continued associating with relatives who tried to silence me whenever I spoke about the issue.
Their actions showed me that they did not truly regret what they had done. They were comfortable maintaining relationships with people who supported and perpetuated the same practice.
The hardest part was not discovering that I had been circumcised. The hardest part was realizing how little concern the people around me had for what had been taken from me.
That realization changed how I viewed my family. Eventually, I chose to cut ties and disappear from their lives. Today, none of my relatives know where I live.

My experience taught me that circumcision is not only about the physical act itself. It is also about the refusal to listen to those who object to what was done to them, even after they find the courage to speak.

name: Drew Walker
content: I was circumsized when I was an infant. I learned about the functions of the foreskin when I was a teenager and confronted my father about his decision to mutilate my body. He brushed it off and said it was done to him as well. This is ritualistic abuse done to so many children for no legitimate reason. I don’t care about someone‘s religion. If your religion compells you to do this to infants or any man then you‘re a monster. If you have this done to your children because it was done to you then you are a coward. I have been restoring my foreskin, and it is a long, tiring process. I want everyone responsible for this practice to hang. I want to see a world where circumsision is eradicated.

name: Tim K
content: I was indifferent to it into early adulthood. I was lightly exposed to what it entails via **Penn & Teller:Bullshit!** My wife joined me in that opposition when she saw how lethargic and damaged infants are after being cut.

My real turning point was being re-introduced to the idea of "restoring". (Bullshit did cover that, but I saw it as a weird thing old men do at the time.) I looked into it for a solid week before starting.
Those early results made things better and I started checking both sides of the circumcision debate.
The more function of foreskin I regained, the better things were for me. I realized I should've had what I gained, and more, for my whole life.
So should others. I also delved more into the risks vs benefits and saw how weak benefits were and how prevalent the damages are.

name: Dare Win
content: I was a young boy when the scar first peeled. I didn't understand what had happened, but I knew instinctively that I didn't like it.

name: Peter Leonhardt
content: When I was 30 years old, I went to a urologist because of a mild case of phimosis. I wanted to try a conservative treatment with a steroid cream. The urologist firmly rejected this option and convinced me that my foreskin had to be removed. He described the procedure as "harmless." The consequences were never discussed by the doctor.
Shortly before the surgery, I wanted to cancel it. However, the doctor spent about 15 minutes persuading me until I finally gave in and consented.
From that moment on, my once happy life turned into an absolute nightmare. I realized I had made a terrible mistake—one that destroyed everything.
**The consequences:**

**Physically:**
👉🏻Loss of what feels like 90% of my sexual sensation and pleasure.
👉🏻Weaker and shorter orgasms.
👉🏻Loss of tactile sensation in my penis.
👉🏻A completely changed perception of my body. I no longer feel comfortable in my own body.

**Psychologically:**
👉🏻Deep sadness.
👉🏻Depression.
👉🏻Loss of all joy in life.
👉🏻Giving up all of my hobbies.
👉🏻Greatly diminished self-esteem.
These are the consequences I now have to live with, while society continues to dismiss and minimize my reality.
At night, before I go to bed, I often wish I could simply fall asleep and never wake up again, so I would no longer have to endure this overwhelming pain.

I live alone and keep my distance from women and relationships because I do not want to burden a partner with my suffering. I see myself as inferior and believe that any woman deserves someone better than a man who is as severely sexually impaired as I am.
I wish I could turn back time and undo something I never believed could happen to me in a developed country.
Today, I know that my mild phimosis could most likely have been treated with a steroid cream or with a foreskin-preserving procedure.
But now it is too late.
A man who was once happy and carefree has become deeply unhappy and bitter, living with this suffering ever since 2020.
My greatest wish is not wealth. It is not money.
My greatest wish is to have my foreskin back.
I would give up everything I own to have it back.

name: Jay Boyd
content: First off, I have gone through an identity crisis and in the worst of times needing to vent online didn't want relatives or those who would run and tell to find what I was saying. For protests I've managed to attend and research I participated in, I typically give the name Jay Boyd.

As an African-American, because of feminism and welfare, my father was ultimately alienated from me so I was raised by a single black mother. He wasn't fully absent though and had a whole host of mental health issues of his own. Ironically it was he who first told me the truth about male genital mutilation in the summer of 2015 per the show [Adam Ruins Everything](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gCSWbTv3hng). He doesn't know it but around the same time I learned I was raped at least once as a child and my so called mom did nothing about it. I cried for months and for the first time since 2009 wanted to commit suicide, but have suffered from depression my entire known life. I was born in 1986 so was 28 at the time. My dad apologized immediately but I quickly learned that it was most likely not his fault and regardless his maturity in accepting the information goes a long way.

I foolishly had not learned the reality of the collective slave mentality of the black community and the extent of child abuse, gaslighting etc. that goes on as a result. Entire books have been written about this so I can't even begin to cover that here. I tried to repair the relationship with my mom only to be told things like get over it, the doctor said you needed it, all men in the world are circumcised blah blah blah. In the worst of times it led to arguments, yelling and me fighting with every fiber in my being to not lose control and kill her in retaliation. Being equally stupid I tried to seek comfort from 3 other female relatives, indicative of the lifelong emasculation to put it simply.
1 just played it off so I never bothered with her again.
2 My older sister among other things said it's not a big deal and "no amount of information will change her mind" - typical black woman, ignorant and proud.

3 Another relative who refused to even watch an educational video, said I'm fixating, me not having my foreskin has nothing to do with me being a man, that I need to be focused on better income and good place to live, my concern is irrational, the proof of men worldwide being intact is "obscure and invalid info" and that my mental health issue related to this is self-imposed. I will not post the screenshot of the communication but am including a few comments from a social media post with some kind, supportive words from female intactivists who (shocker) are European.

There is a pattern here, that I had to learn the hard and brutal way, ultimately destroying my trust in black women and later virtually all black people. It's relevant because many things slave owners used to do to us, we now do to each other. Also, Europeans didn't start the enslavement of Africans but fought and died to end most of it. To the black woman it's always white man bad but never Islam.

There's no way I can even begin to briefly explain how much my life was destroyed before I even got to figure out who I am, nor the consequences of all the abuse I've endured. I do know I was suicidal since before the teen years, but largely suffered in silence, a practice which I sadly learned in my 30's was the best choice given the circumstances. Even so called mental health professionals refuse to take our MGM grief seriously.

My memory is fuzzy and I don't remember how old I was but it was before puberty. At some point I recalled an experience that might've been a [MGM PTSD](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22114254/) re-enactment. One day I started feeling serious lower abdominal area pains, and I remember screaming for what felt like hours but who really knows. Eventually, 911 was called and I was taken to a poison control center but either en route or shortly after arriving the pain stopped. I don't remember anything after that. If this pain did in fact go on for hours, considering it could've been an appendix burst, it's just more proof my family does not care about me.

Few know this but in terms of consensual sex I'm actually a virgin. I went through a period of sexual confusion up until around the age of 23 then after a brief period of hypersexual desires which I'm grateful I didn't act on knowing the root causes now I lost all desire before the age of 25. I’m 39 now and have settled on calling this trauma based asexuality. Today, even if I found myself comfortable with attempting to enter a romantic relationship, I don't have the confidence to be the father any potential children would deserve. I certainly wouldn't allow any to be born in this country.

The only thing that's kept me from ending my life from 2015 onward has been that I stumbled across the futurist movement first, which includes indefinite healthy human lifespans. Combined with the promise of time travel, it's the only hope I have to one day go back and save myself or at least counteract what I should've never had to endure.
Friday, July 20th, 2018 was the first time I attended a protest. It was with the [Bloodstained Men](https://www.bloodstainedmen.com/) in Wilmington, Delaware.

With so many things at play, by 2021 my mental health took a deep nose dive and I didn't have it in my to attend any more protests but in 2026 things finally began to improve and I made a surprise return, for the first time outside of my hometown metro area, at the [Genital Integrity Awareness Week](https://www.giaw.org/) in Washington DC. I was only there for a day but it's a start.

I kept any obvious picture or video of me out of this, only because I regrettably still deal with relatives despite it being for good reasons and I want to minimize the risk of being confronted because the time to repair our relationship has long gone, with so far well controlled anger and rage being the only thing left so it's not a fear of conflict but rather to keep the monster they created in me at bay.
